Purpose

Short guidance for Copilot sessions: build/test/lint commands, high-level architecture, and conventions specific to this repository.

Build / test / lint (exact commands)

- Build (Nix-driven):
  - make build  # equivalent to: nix build
- Run full test suite:
  - go test ./...
- Run tests for this repo's packages (example single-package):
  - go test ./pkg        # run tests only in pkg
  - go test ./pkg/...    # run tests in pkg and subpackages
- Lint / format / CI checks:
  - nix flake check      # runs actionlint, nixfmt, gofmt via treefmt
- Tidying / dependency sync:
  - go mod tidy
  - gomod2nix generate --outdir ./nix
- Update Nix flake inputs:
  - make update          # or: nix flake update
- Build pipeline artifacts individually (Nix):
  - nix build .#tools
  - nix build .#openapi
  - nix build .#config
  - nix build .#spec
  - nix build .#provider
- CI (GitHub Actions): workflow runs `nix flake check` then `nix build .#`

High-level architecture (big-picture)

- Purpose: generate a Terraform provider for pfSense from pfSense's REST OpenAPI spec using a Nix-driven pipeline. No provider code is handwritten — everything is generated by the pipeline.

- Nix-driven code generation pipeline (high level):
  1. Build the repository's CLI tools (cmd/) via nix/tools.nix.
  2. Fetch upstream parsing helpers and copy into internal/config/config.go (generated by nix/upstream.nix).
  3. Fetch pfSense OpenAPI JSON and run patch-openapi to flatten allOfs (nix/openapi.nix → pkg/PatchSpec).
  4. Generate the tfplugingen-openapi config.yaml from the patched spec (nix/config.nix → pkg/GenerateConfig).
  5. Produce schema.json via genProviderSpec (nix/provider-spec.nix).
  6. Generate provider Go source via genProvider (nix/provider.nix). Compilation into a final provider binary is not yet complete.

- Go code responsibilities:
  - cmd/patch-openapi: PatchSpec entrypoint (pkg.PatchSpec) — flattens allOf entries and bundles spec.
  - cmd/gen-config: GenerateConfig entrypoint (pkg.GenerateConfig) — builds generator config YAML.
  - cmd/slurp-source: ExtractConfig entrypoint.
  - pkg/openapi.go: spec flattening logic (PatchSpec).
  - pkg/config.go: builds config.Config from the OpenAPI model (GenerateConfig + ConfigFor()).
  - internal/config/config.go: GENERATED (from upstream HashiCorp code); do not edit manually.

Key repository conventions

- internal/config/config.go is generated by the Nix pipeline (do not edit).
- To add or change a resource mapping for the generator:
  - Edit pkg/config.go → ConfigFor() and add entries to the Resources map. Each Resource uses OpenApiSpecLocation { Path, Method } for CRUD operations.
  - After edits, run `nix build` (or `make build`) to run the pipeline and regenerate downstream artifacts.
- Generator config expectations:
  - OpenApiSpecLocation.Path matches OpenAPI path keys (e.g., "/api/v2/firewall/rule").
  - Method is HTTP method string (GET/POST/etc.).
  - Attribute-location strings are dot-separated (e.g., category.tags.name) — config validation enforces this pattern.
- Tests & world abstraction:
  - Code uses github.com/unmango/go/world for OS abstraction in tests; use world.FromContext(ctx).Os() helpers when testing I/O behavior.
- Dependency management:
  - gomod2nix is used to sync go.mod -> nix/gomod2nix.toml for reproducible Nix builds.
- CI expectations:
  - GitHub Actions run nix flake check and nix build; ensure nix tooling is available in CI.

Files to consult

- AGENTS.md (in repo root) — contains the above pipeline and commands; use it as the authoritative on-repo agent guidance.
- pkg/config.go — example ConfigFor implementation and where to add Resources.
- pkg/openapi.go / pkg/patch code — spec flattening logic.
- Makefile — convenient targets: build, update, check, tidy.

How Copilot sessions should behave here

- Prefer editing pkg/config.go for generator changes.
- Never propose manual edits to internal/config/config.go — it's generated and overwritten by the pipeline.
- When suggesting commands, prefer the Nix-based ones (make build / nix build / nix flake check) to ensure reproducible outputs.

If you want further coverage

If there are areas to expand (example: contributor workflow for local provider testing, or step-by-step for generating the provider binary locally), say which area and Copilot will add a short section.
